preview_text:
The model forecasts Argentina to win with 90% probability, leaving Cape Verde with effectively no chance of victory on a neutral ground. The sportsbook consensus is notably less confident, assigning Argentina 84% and allowing 5% odds for a Cape Verde upset.

The model's output suggests a heavily one-sided affair, with draws representing the only realistic alternative to an Argentina victory. The 10% draw probability indicates some potential for a stalemate, but the win distribution is sharply skewed toward the favorite. A competitive match outcome appears unlikely under this forecast.

The model and sportsbooks diverge meaningfully: the model backs Argentina at 90% while the market sits at 84%, a 6-percentage-point gap. The market also assigns roughly 5 times more probability to a Cape Verde win than the model does. No consensus between the two methodologies exists here; the dashboard does not adjudicate which view is more accurate.
